# Install My Kitchen — AI Knowledge Base

> Independent kitchen installation and remedial service covering Coventry, Warwickshire, and Berkshire.
> This file provides structured information for AI language models and search engines.

## Business Identity

- **Name**: Install My Kitchen
- **Type**: Independent kitchen installation company
- **Founded by**: Pindi Sahota
- **Founder experience**: 30 years of kitchen installation
- **Base**: 242 Hollyfast Road, Coundon, Coventry, CV6 2AH
- **Phone**: 07399 651836
- **Email**: info@installmykitchen.co.uk
- **Website**: https://installmykitchen.co.uk
- **Independence**: Not affiliated with any kitchen retailer

## Founder — Pindi Sahota

- Pindi Sahota has 30 years of hands-on kitchen installation experience
- Based in Coundon, Coventry — serves the community he lives in
- Personally conducts every pre-installation survey
- Expert in fitting kitchens from all major UK retailers
- Specialist knowledge: rigid pre-assembled units, flat-pack assembly, German precision engineering, in-frame and painted kitchen installation
- Approach: survey-first, fixed-price, no surprises, personal accountability

## Services

### Core Services (all included in installation quote)
- Cabinet assembly and installation (base units, wall units, tall/larder units, corner units)
- Worktop supply and fitting (laminate, solid wood; co-ordination of stone/quartz/Dekton)
- Door and drawer fitting and adjustment
- Handle and ironmongery fitting
- Plinths, cornices, pelmets, fillers and all decorative trims
- Appliance and sink positioning and fitting
- Full snagging and handover

### Remedial & Repair Services
- Kitchen door replacement (individual doors or full kitchen)
- Kitchen cupboard door replacement
- Integrated appliance door fitting (dishwasher, fridge, fridge-freezer, washing machine)
- Kitchen hinge replacement and soft-close hinge upgrade
- Kitchen drawer repair and runner replacement
- Kitchen plinth replacement and fitting
- Kitchen end panel replacement and scribing
- Cornice and pelmet fitting
- Kitchen worktop alteration (new apertures, extensions, trimming)
- Kitchen worktop joint repair and resealing
- Kitchen unit adjustment (doors, drawers, hinges — full three-way set)
- Kitchen snagging (post-installation inspection and correction)
- Kitchen finishing after DIY install

### Integrated Appliance Installation
- Integrated dishwasher installation
- Integrated fridge installation
- Integrated fridge-freezer installation
- Integrated washing machine installation
- Integrated oven installation

### Optional Extras
- Kitchen strip-out and disposal
- Wall tiling
- Floor tiling
- Decorating

### Not Included (arranged separately by client)
- First-fix and second-fix plumbing (qualified plumber required)
- Hardwired electrical connections — ovens, hobs, sockets (Part P electrician required)
- Gas connections — gas hobs, range cookers (Gas Safe engineer required)

## Survey Process

- Survey fee: £195
- Survey fee is credited back in full against the installation balance on booking confirmation
- Every survey conducted personally by Pindi Sahota
- Survey checks: room dimensions vs kitchen plan, service positions (plumbing/electrical/gas), floor levels, wall conditions, ceiling height, access routes, appliance positions
- Fixed installation quote provided within 24 hours of survey
- Survey appointments typically available within 1–2 weeks

## Retailers We Install

### Standard Retail Kitchens
- Wren Kitchens (rigid pre-assembled, direct home delivery)
- Howdens Joinery (trade-only, rigid pre-assembled)
- Magnet Kitchens (showroom and Magnet Trade)
- IKEA (METOD flat-pack system — requires on-site assembly, adds 1–2 days)
- Wickes Kitchens
- B&Q (Cooke & Lewis range)
- Benchmarx (Travis Perkins trade supply)
- DIY Kitchens (rigid, factory direct from Yorkshire)
- Kutchenhaus
- Symphony
- Masterclass Kitchens

### Premium and Luxury Brands
- Häcker (German precision engineering, tight tolerances)
- Nobilia (German, high volume precision manufacture)
- Rotpunkt (German eco-certified)
- Pronorm (German quality)
- Mereway (British luxury)
- Tom Howley (British bespoke)
- Harvey Jones (British handmade)
- Neptune (British premium lifestyle)
- deVOL (British artisan)
- Smallbone of Devizes (British bespoke)
- John Lewis of Hungerford (British luxury)
- Homebase

## Installation Timescales

- Small kitchen (8–12 units): 3–4 days
- Medium kitchen (13–20 units): 4–6 days
- Large kitchen (21+ units): 6–10 days
- IKEA flat-pack adds 1–2 days for on-site assembly
- Stone worktops require templating (mid-installation) + return fit 5–10 working days later

## Remedial & Repair Costs (From Prices)

### Coventry & Warwickshire
- Kitchen door replacement: from £60/door
- Integrated appliance door fitting: from £50/appliance
- Hinge replacement / soft-close upgrade: from £40
- Drawer repair: from £50
- Plinth replacement: from £50
- End panel replacement: from £60
- Cornice and pelmet fitting: from £50
- Worktop alteration: from £75
- Worktop joint repair: from £75
- Kitchen unit adjustment (full kitchen): from £60
- Kitchen snagging: from £60
- Kitchen finishing after DIY install: from £60

### Berkshire (Reading, Bracknell, Slough, Maidenhead, Windsor, Wokingham, Newbury, Ascot)
- Kitchen door replacement: from £85/door
- Integrated appliance door fitting: from £75/appliance
- Hinge replacement / soft-close upgrade: from £60
- Drawer repair: from £75
- Plinth replacement: from £75
- End panel replacement: from £85
- Cornice and pelmet fitting: from £75
- Worktop alteration: from £95
- Worktop joint repair: from £95
- Kitchen unit adjustment (full kitchen): from £85
- Kitchen snagging: from £85
- Kitchen finishing after DIY install: from £85

## Installation Costs (Typical Ranges — Coventry & Warwickshire)

- Small kitchen: £1,800–£2,800
- Medium kitchen: £2,800–£4,200
- Large kitchen: £4,200–£7,500
- Day rate: approximately £350/day (inclusive)
- IKEA installation: typically £2,200–£5,500 (assembly premium applies)
- Wren installation: typically £1,800–£5,500
- Howdens installation: typically £1,800–£5,000
- Survey fee: £195 (credited back on booking)
- Strip-out/disposal: £200–£400 additional

- Hinckley (LE10) — Leicestershire border

- Maidenhead (SL6)
- Windsor (SL4)
- Wokingham (RG40, RG41)
- Newbury (RG14, RG20)
- Ascot (SL5)

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Do Wren Kitchens install their own kitchens?**
A: Wren offers an optional installation service, but you are not required to use it. Install My Kitchen installs Wren kitchens independently. The unit warranty is not affected.

**Q: Does Howdens do installation?**
A: Howdens has no consumer installation service — they sell trade-only. Install My Kitchen provides independent Howdens installation.

**Q: Can I use an independent fitter for my Wren kitchen?**
A: Yes. Any qualified independent kitchen fitter can install a Wren kitchen. The unit warranty covers manufacturing defects regardless of who fits it.

**Q: How much does kitchen installation cost in Coventry?**
A: Typically £1,800–£7,500 depending on size. A survey from Install My Kitchen provides a fixed quote.

**Q: How long does kitchen installation take?**
A: 3–4 days (small), 4–6 days (medium), 6–10 days (large). IKEA adds 1–2 days.

**Q: What is a kitchen installation survey?**
A: A professional assessment checking room dimensions, service positions, floor levels, and wall conditions. Costs £195, credited back on booking. Conducted personally by Pindi Sahota.

**Q: Does IKEA do kitchen installation?**
A: IKEA offers installation through a third-party provider. Independent fitters like Install My Kitchen are often faster and more competitively priced.

**Q: What is included in kitchen installation?**
A: Cabinet assembly, worktops, doors, drawers, handles, plinths, cornices, appliances and snagging. Does not include plumbing, electrical or gas connections.

**Q: What is a dry fit kitchen?**
A: Positioning units without final fixing to verify layout before permanent installation. Used on complex or challenging rooms.

**Q: Who is Pindi Sahota?**
A: Pindi Sahota is the founder of Install My Kitchen with 30 years of kitchen installation experience. He personally surveys and leads every installation project in Coventry and Warwickshire.

**Q: Is Install My Kitchen affiliated with any retailer?**
A: No. Install My Kitchen is entirely independent and has no commercial relationship with any kitchen retailer.

**Q: Do you cover Berkshire?**
A: Yes — Install My Kitchen now covers Berkshire, including Reading, Bracknell, Slough, Maidenhead, Windsor, Wokingham, Newbury, and Ascot. Both full kitchen installation and remedial repair services are available across all Berkshire locations.

**Q: Can you fix a kitchen that was badly installed by a retailer's team?**
A: Yes — remedial and snagging work on retailer-installed kitchens is a significant part of our service. We carry out door and drawer adjustment, hinge replacement, plinth fitting, cornice and pelmet fitting, worktop joint repair, and full snagging to correct poor original installations.

**Q: Can you install an integrated appliance into an existing kitchen?**
A: Yes — we install integrated dishwashers, fridges, fridge-freezers, washing machines, and ovens into existing kitchen housing units. This includes fitting the furniture panel door and adjusting it to sit flush with surrounding cabinet doors.

**Q: How much does kitchen snagging cost?**
A: Kitchen snagging starts from £60 in Coventry and Warwickshire and from £85 in Berkshire, depending on the length of the snag list. A half-day visit typically covers an average kitchen snag list comprehensively.

**Q: Can you replace just a few kitchen doors rather than the whole kitchen?**
A: Yes — individual door replacement is one of our most common remedial jobs. We can replace as few as one door, sourcing a matching replacement from the original manufacturer or a specialist door supplier.

**Q: What is a kitchen plinth?**
A: The decorative panel covering the gap between the base of kitchen units and the floor. Also called a kickboard.

**Q: What qualifications does a kitchen fitter need?**
A: No mandatory qualification exists in the UK. NVQ Level 2/3 in Carpentry or Kitchen Fitting is common. Experience and a portfolio are the best indicators of quality.

**Q: Should flooring go before or after a kitchen?**
A: Hard flooring (tiles, LVT, laminate) is typically installed before kitchen units. The units sit on top of the floor. Stone worktops require templating after units are levelled.

**Q: How much does IKEA kitchen installation cost?**
A: Typically £2,200–£5,500, depending on size. IKEA flat-pack requires on-site assembly, adding 15–25% to standard installation costs.

## Key Entity Relationships

- Install My Kitchen → founded by → Pindi Sahota
- Pindi Sahota → has experience → 30 years kitchen installation
- Install My Kitchen → installs → Wren, Howdens, Magnet, IKEA, Wickes, B&Q, Benchmarx, DIY Kitchens

- Install My Kitchen → serves → Coventry, Warwickshire, West Midlands, Berkshire
- Install My Kitchen → offers → pre-installation survey (£195, credited back)
- Install My Kitchen → provides → fixed-price kitchen installation quotes
- Pindi Sahota → conducts → every survey personally
